title: Workflow
text:
CORE transforms heterogeneous raw data into transparent, reviewable, reproducible, and analysis-ready outputs through a sequence of explicit methodological stages.
Rather than combining multiple operations within a single opaque procedure, each stage performs a distinct scientific responsibility while preserving the intermediate information required to understand how final entity resolution decisions have been obtained.
The workflow therefore represents more than a computational pipeline.
It provides a structured research process in which data preparation, computational evaluation, expert review, knowledge preservation, and output generation remain clearly separated while contributing to a coherent and reproducible methodology.

title: Workflow

title: Workflow
text:
Passive Learning
Validated decisions are preserved but do not influence computational procedures.

title: Workflow
text:
Consensus Learning
Knowledge becomes eligible for methodological influence only after sufficient independent scientific validation has accumulated.

title: Workflow
text:
Soft Learning
Validated knowledge influences future workflows through transparent score adjustments and review prioritisation rather than automatic acceptance or rejection.
This approach protects future investigations from isolated reviewer errors while preserving cumulative scientific knowledge.

title: Workflow
text:
Sequential
Each stage builds explicitly upon the outputs of the previous stage.

title: Workflow
text:
Transparent
Intermediate outputs remain available throughout the workflow.

title: Workflow
text:
Reviewable
Scientific judgement remains an explicit component of the workflow through the Review Queue.

title: Workflow
text:
Reproducible
The workflow can be repeated and independently verified across different datasets and projects.

title: Workflow
text:
Cumulative
Validated knowledge contributes to successive investigations rather than being repeatedly reconstructed.

title: Philosophy
text:
Traditional entity resolution workflows often treat matching as a temporary preprocessing task whose only objective is to produce an analysis-ready dataset.
Once matching has been completed, the dataset survives but the reasoning, assumptions, and expert judgement required to construct it frequently disappear with the project itself.
CORE adopts a fundamentally different perspective.

Entity resolution does not merely produce analytical datasets.
It produces scientific knowledge.

The framework has therefore been designed around the idea that validated entity resolution decisions should be preserved, documented, and made available for future empirical research rather than discarded at the conclusion of individual projects.

title: Philosophy

title: Methodology
Methodology
Methodology
text:
Transparency
Every important methodological decision remains observable and open to scientific inspection.
Researchers are able to inspect intermediate outputs, review decisions, and understand how final correspondences were established.

title: Methodology
text:
Scientific Judgement
Computational procedures support expert reasoning but do not replace it whenever uncertainty remains.
Scientific judgement therefore remains an explicit component of the framework rather than an undocumented correction performed outside the software.

title: Methodology
text:
Reproducibility
Entity resolution decisions are documented in a manner that allows independent verification and future replication.
Reproducibility within CORE extends beyond producing identical analytical datasets.
The framework seeks to preserve the reasoning through which those datasets were constructed.

title: Methodology
text:
Knowledge Preservation
Validated scientific judgement is preserved beyond individual projects so that future investigations may benefit from previous empirical evaluation.
Knowledge generated during entity resolution therefore becomes a reusable scientific asset rather than a temporary by-product of data preparation.

title: Methodology
text:
Methodological Continuity
Entity resolution is regarded as a cumulative research activity in which validated knowledge contributes to successive investigations rather than being repeatedly reconstructed.
Scientific knowledge therefore accumulates across projects rather than disappearing at the conclusion of individual studies.

title: Methodology
text:
Passive Learning
Validated decisions are preserved but do not influence computational procedures.

title: Methodology
text:
Consensus Learning
Knowledge becomes eligible for methodological influence only after sufficient independent scientific validation has accumulated.

title: Methodology
text:
Soft Learning
Validated knowledge influences future workflows through transparent score adjustments and review prioritisation rather than automatic acceptance or rejection.
This approach protects future investigations from isolated reviewer errors while preserving the cumulative development of scientific knowledge.

title: Corporate Entity Resolution Engine (CORE)
text:
Entity resolution does not merely produce analytical datasets.
It produces scientific knowledge.






Try CORE directly in your browser.


Launch Live Demonstrator


CORE is an open-source research software framework designed to support transparent, reviewable, reproducible, and cumulative entity resolution in empirical research.
Rather than treating entity resolution as a disposable data-cleaning task, CORE approaches entity resolution as a scientific process in which computational methods, expert judgement, and validated knowledge work together to produce trustworthy analytical datasets.
Originally developed for corporate entity resolution, CORE has been intentionally designed as a general methodology capable of supporting transparent entity resolution across a broad range of empirical research domains.

Originally developed for corporate entity resolution, CORE has been intentionally designed as a general methodology capable of supporting transparent entity resolution across a broad range of empirical research domains.

title: Corporate Entity Resolution Engine (CORE)
Corporate Entity Resolution Engine (CORE)
text:
Core Principles
CORE has been designed around five methodological principles:

Transparency
Scientific Judgement
Reproducibility
Knowledge Preservation
Methodological Continuity

Together, these principles establish entity resolution as a documented and reproducible scientific activity rather than a project-specific preprocessing task.

title: Corporate Entity Resolution Engine (CORE)
text:
Framework Components
The CORE ecosystem currently consists of six major components:







Component
Purpose




Entity Resolution Engine
Computational entity resolution workflow


Review Queue
Transparent expert evaluation of uncertain matches


Learning Layer
Preservation of validated review history


Dictionary Infrastructure
Reusable project and community knowledge


Documentation Portal
Methodological and architectural documentation


Research Software Infrastructure
Reproducibility and software quality support

title: Corporate Entity Resolution Engine (CORE)
text:
Methodological Workflow
CORE implements a six-stage research workflow.
Raw Data
    ↓
Data Acquisition
    ↓
Data Preparation
    ↓
Entity Resolution
    ↓
Expert Validation
    ↓
Knowledge Preservation
    ↓
Analysis-Ready Outputs

title: CORE Demonstrator
CORE Demonstrator
CORE Demonstrator
Installation
text:
This page explains how to install and configure CORE.
CORE is distributed as an open-source research software framework and can be installed directly from the GitHub repository.


Requirements
CORE currently requires:

R (>= 4.3 recommended)
RStudio (recommended)
Git
Internet access for package installation

The framework has been developed and tested on Windows, Linux, and macOS.



Step 1 — Clone the Repository
Clone the repository from GitHub:
```bash git clone https://github.com/yrtsv/CORE.git

title: Tutorials
text:
This section provides practical examples demonstrating how CORE can be used within empirical research workflows.
The objective of these tutorials is not only to explain how the software operates, but also to illustrate the methodological principles underlying transparent and reproducible entity resolution.


Tutorial 1 — Running the CORE Demonstrator
The easiest way to explore CORE is through the interactive Shiny demonstrator.
Start the application from the project root directory:
```r options(shiny.launch.browser = TRUE)
shiny::runApp( appDir = “app”, host = “127.0.0.1”, port = 3838, launch.browser = TRUE )
